name: Nether Wallop
overview: Nether Wallop is a Test Valley village in Hampshire, one of the three 'Wallops' (with Over and Middle Wallop) linked by the Wallop Brook, whose name derives from Old English for 'the valley of springing water'. It is a thriving community of predominantly period and thatched properties, with the Anglo-Saxon church of St Andrew (noted for rare medieval wall paintings), a primary school and a village hall. The neighbouring Wallops share pubs and a shop/Post Office, and the market town of Stockbridge is about 5 miles away.
character: A picturesque, historic Test Valley village of thatched cottages around the Wallop Brook, quiet and rural yet within reach of Stockbridge and good rail links.
amenities: St Andrew's Church (Anglo-Saxon, with medieval wall paintings)
Shop/Post Office and pubs in neighbouring Over/Middle Wallop

schools.name: Secondary schools in Stockbridge/Andover area (e.g. Test Valley School)

commutes.notes: Via Grateley (~4 miles) or Andover stations

prices.summary: A desirable Test Valley village; thatched and period cottages plus larger listed houses and barn conversions command strong values, with limited supply. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: St Andrew's Church and its medieval wall paintings
Walks along the Wallop Brook and Test Valley
Trout fishing on the River Test
Boutique shopping and dining in Stockbridge
placesToEat: Pubs in Over and Middle Wallop
Restaurants and pubs in nearby Stockbridge
pros: Good-rated primary school and Anglo-Saxon church
Test Valley setting with excellent walking and fishing
Mainline rail at Grateley/Andover within reach
cons: Few amenities in Nether Wallop itself (shop/pubs are in neighbouring Wallops)
No railway station in the village
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking a quintessential, peaceful Test Valley village with a strong primary school and period character, within reach of Stockbridge.
primarySupermarket: Village garage/store and shop in Over Wallop; supermarkets in Stockbridge/Andover
